title: Ho Yinsen
content:
{{CharHub|Ho Yinsen|Ho Yinsen}} {{Character |image = Yinsen.jpg |real name = Ho Yinsen (هو یینسن) |species = [[Human]] |citizenship = {{Citizenship|AFGH}} |gender = Male |title = Doctor |affiliation = [[University of Cambridge]] (formerly) |DOD = [[2008|May 2008]]{{Ref|IM}}{{Ref|MSTMCUAOT}} |movie = ''[[Iron Man (film)|Iron Man]]''
''[[Iron Man 3]]'' (flashbacks) |game = ''[[Iron Man: Mark III Suit Test]]'' |comic = ''[[Iron Man: I Am Iron Man!]]'' |book = ''[[Iron Man Read-Along Storybook and CD]]''
''[[Avengers: Infinity War: The Heroes' Journey]]'' (flashbacks) |actor = [[Shaun Toub]] |status = Deceased}} {{Quote|What you just saw... That is your legacy, Stark. Your life's work in the hands of [[Ten Rings (Organization)|those murderers]]. Is that how you want to go out? Is this the last act of defiance of the great Tony Stark? Or are you going to do something about it?|Ho Yinsen to [[Tony Stark]]|Iron Man (film)}} Doctor '''Ho Yinsen''' (Dari: '''هو یینسن''') was a renowned scientist and surgeon who hailed from the village of [[Gulmira]]. With his family being killed during a surge in local terrorist activity, Yinsen was captured by the [[Ten Rings (Organization)|Ten Rings]] and held captive in the Afghan mountains. When [[Tony Stark]] was also [[Kidnapping of Tony Stark|kidnapped]], Yinsen was charged with attending to Stark's medical needs after he was brought in injured from an explosive. After several months in captivity, Yinsen helped Stark escape the cave by developing a [[Iron Man Armor: Mark I|suit of armor]], but willingly sacrificed himself in the process to the terrorists, telling Stark not to waste his life before he died of his injuries. ==Biography== {{MD|Ho Yinsen (Earth-199999)}} ===Early Life=== ====Meeting Tony Stark==== {{Quote|Mr. Stark, Ho Yinsen."
"Ah, I finally met a man called 'Ho'.|Ho Yinsen and [[Tony Stark]]|Iron Man 3}} [[File:Ho Yinsen meets Tony Stark.png|{{AltFloat}}|Ho Yinsen introduces himself to [[Tony Stark]]]] Ho Yinsen was an [[Afghanistan|Afghan]] man from the small village of [[Gulmira]]. Eventually, he became a scientist after graduating from [[University of Cambridge]] and started to work in [[Europe]].{{Ref|IM}} Yinsen met [[Tony Stark]] on New Year's Eve in [[1999]] at [[Bern 2000]] in [[Bern]], [[Switzerland]], after Stark delivered a lecture, while very drunk, on integrated circuits. Yinsen tried to introduce him and [[Maya Hansen]] to [[Wu]], but Stark drunkenly brushed him off to focus his attention on Hansen, joking that he had finally met a man named Ho. Though somewhat disappointed, Yinsen resigned himself to hopefully meeting Stark properly at another time.{{Ref|IM3}} ===Ten Rings' Hostage=== ====Saving Tony Stark's Life==== [[File:YinsenTonyTenRings.png|{{AltFloat}}|Yinsen works as translator for the [[Ten Rings (Organization)|Ten Rings]]]] {{Quote|What the hell did you do to me?"
"What I did? What I did is to save your life. I removed all the shrapnel I could, but there's a lot left.|[[Tony Stark]] and Ho Yinsen|Iron Man (film)}} [[2008|Nine years later]], Yinsen was captured by the [[Ten Rings (Organization)|Ten Rings]], a terrorist group in [[Afghanistan]]. When the Ten Rings brought in [[Tony Stark]], injured from a [[Stark Industries Fragmentation Shell|missile]] used in their [[Kidnapping of Tony Stark|ambush]], they charged Yinsen with keeping him alive. A skilled and resourceful field surgeon, Yinsen removed much of the shrapnel from Stark's body and helped keep him alive long enough to build an electromagnet piece in his chest to keep the remaining shrapnel from entering his heart.{{Ref|IM}} ====Partners in Captivity==== [[File:TonywithHoYinsen-IM.png|{{AltFloat}}|Yinsen building the [[Iron Man Armor: Mark I|Mark I]] with [[Tony Stark]]]] {{Quote|Why should I do anything? [[Ten Rings (Organization)|They]]'re going to kill me, you, either way. And if they don't, I'll probably be dead in a week."
"Well then, this is a very important week for you, isn't it?|[[Tony Stark]] and Ho Yinsen|Iron Man (film)}} Under captivity, [[Tony Stark]] and Yinsen were ordered by the [[Ten Rings (Organization)|Ten Rings]] to build a [[Jericho]] missile, or they would be killed. However, Stark refused and was tortured. Yinsen managed to motivate him and both men acknowledged that they would be murdered either way and instead focused on an escape plan. Stark revealed to Yinsen that he would use the parts that the Ten Rings gave them and build a [[Iron Man Armor: Mark I|weaponized suit of armor]], which he would use to take out the terrorists and return Yinsen to his family.{{Ref|IM}} ====Escape and Death==== [[File:Stark and Yinsen.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Yinsen fits [[Tony Stark]] into the [[Iron Man Armor: Mark I|Mark I Armor]]]] {{Quote|This was always the plan, Stark."
"Come on, you're going to go see your family. Get up."
"My family is dead. I'm going to see them now, Stark. It's okay, I want this.|Ho Yinsen and [[Tony Stark]]|Iron Man (film)}} Yinsen helped [[Tony Stark]] keep the [[Iron Man Armor: Mark I|Mark I Armor]] he was making a secret from [[Raza Hamidmi al-Wazar]] and assisted in its creation. When Stark asked about Yinsen's family, Yinsen merely noted that he would see them when he left the [[Ten Rings Base]]. Yinsen also pointed out that Stark's legacy was his weapons being used by the [[Ten Rings (Organization)|Ten Rings]] and suggests that he should rectify this. [[File:Ho Yinsen.png|{{AltFloat}}|Yinsen gives [[Tony Stark]] advice before dying]] During the escape the terrorists were alerted to their plan, seeing there was no way they could power up the suit while defending themselves simultaneously, Yinsen sacrificed himself by taking a gun and attacking the terrorists, thereby giving Stark time to power up the suit. He was mortally wounded when he ran around a corner only to be shot by dozens of terrorists. When Stark found Yinsen, bleeding out from his wounds, he reassured Stark that his demise had always been the plan as his family was dead and that he wished to be reunited with them. Stark thanked Yinsen for saving him. With his dying breath, Yinsen asked Stark not to waste the rest of his life. Stark was quietly enraged by Yinsen's death, and dispatched the remaining Ten Rings members with emphatic fury.{{Ref|IM}} ===Legacy=== ====Remembered by Tony Stark==== {{Quote|Thank you for saving me."
"Don't waste it. Don't waste your life.|[[Tony Stark]] and Ho Yinsen|Iron Man (film)}} Thanks to Yinsen's [[Kidnapping of Tony Stark#Escape|sacrifice]], [[Tony Stark]] was able to escape and once back in the [[United States]], he discontinued [[Stark Industries]]' mass weapon production line despite this causing his company's stock market value to plummet, much to [[Obadiah Stane]]'s chagrin. After discovering that Stane was the one responsible for his kidnapping and [[Duel of Los Angeles|defeating]] Stane, Stark dedicated himself to becoming a crimefighter, taking on the identity of Iron Man, for which he designed similar [[:Category:Iron Man Armors|armors]] to the one he and Yinsen designed on [[Afghanistan]].{{Ref|IM}} Despite meeting Yinsen for a short time, Stark never forgot his advice and dedicated the rest of his life to become less egotistical and become more selfless and heroic. As the years passed by, Stark came to start [[Pepper Potts|a]] [[Morgan Stark|family]] of his own just like Yinsen once did. This eventually culminated in Stark [[Battle of Earth|sacrificing himself]] to stop [[Thanos/Cosmic Time Heist|Thanos]] from destroying the universe in [[2023]], finally giving up his life like Yinsen did for him in the past.{{Ref|AE}} ==Abilities== {{Quote|That is an electromagnet. Hooked up to a car battery. And it's keeping the shrapnel from entering your heart.|Ho Yinsen to [[Tony Stark]]|Iron Man (film)}} *'''Genius-Level Intellect''': Yinsen was a very smart and helpful man. When he first met [[Tony Stark]], they were both at a [[Bern 2000|New Year's Party]] with many other geniuses. He tried to approach Stark, but was shot down. He also graduated from [[University of Cambridge]]. He was also a very logical man, he taught Stark the lesson of how his own decisions have consequences on people. He told Stark not to waste his life. This is what ultimately led to Iron Man and his quest until his death. *'''Master Engineer''': [[File:Palladium 1.png|thumb|250px|Yinsen and [[Tony Stark]] build an [[Arc Reactor]]]] While saving [[Tony Stark]], Yinsen managed to connect wires to a car battery and then make an electromagnet within Stark's chest so that the shrapnel was kept from entering his heart and killing him. His engineering gave Stark the idea to create the first [[Arc Reactor]], which Yinsen helped with. He also helped Stark create the [[Iron Man Armor: Mark I|prototype Iron Man Armor]]. *'''Expert Scientist''': Yinsen was a renowned scientist before being captured by the [[Ten Rings (Organization)|Ten Rings]]. He even graduated from [[University of Cambridge]] with a degree. {{Quote|Why do you think you're still alive, huh?|Ho Yinsen to [[Tony Stark]]|Iron Man (film)}} *'''Master Physician''': Yinsen was considered a world-renowned surgeon and was able to keep [[Tony Stark]] alive when he had shrapnel from a [[Stark Industries Fragmentation Shell|missile]] digging into his heart, by improvising an electromagnet with the few resources he had in captivity, and keep the shrapnel of his heart. {{Quote|How many languages do you speak?"
"A lot.|[[Tony Stark]] and Ho Yinsen|Iron Man (film)}} *'''Multilingualism''': Yinsen was fluent in his native Dari, as well as English, Russian, Urdu, French, and a little Hungarian. ==Equipment== ===Weapons=== *'''{{WPS|Heckler & Koch G36|Heckler & Koch G36C}}''': Yinsen grabbed one of these rifles from one of the members of the [[Ten Rings (Organization)|Ten Rings]] during their escape attempt and started to shoot wildly in order to divert the attention from [[Tony Stark]] while he finished preparing his [[Iron Man Armor: Mark I|armor]] for the escape. ===Other Equipment=== *'''[[Iron Man Armor: Mark I]]''': In an attempt to escape captivity, Yinsen and [[Tony Stark]] worked together to building an armor which Stark could wear by tricking the [[Ten Rings (Organization)|Ten Rings]] into thinking they were recreating the [[Jericho]] missile. Stark donned the armor and used it to attempt to help Yinsen escape, but Yinsen died in the process. *'''[[Arc Reactor]]''': To save Stark from getting shrapnel near his heart, Yinsen implanted a device into Stark's chest using an electromagnet and a car battery, a feat that that saved his life. Later, Stark used the model for the Arc Reactor to replace the car battery with a stronger one. ==Facilities== *'''[[Ten Rings Base]]''': Yinsen was held captive in the [[Ten Rings (Organization)|Ten Rings]]' base in [[Afghanistan]], where his skills were used to help [[Tony Stark]] build a [[Jericho]] missile for [[Raza Hamidmi al-Wazar]], and translate [[Abu Bakaar]]'s words for Stark. Yinsen aided Stark in building the [[Iron Man Armor: Mark I|Mark I Armor]], and [[Kidnapping of Tony Stark|escaping]] from the base despite his death. ==Relationships== {{Col-begin}}{{Col-2}} ===Family=== *Wife † *Child † ===Allies=== *[[Wu]] - Associate *[[Jan Georgoh]] *[[Stark Industries]] **[[Tony Stark]] † - Friend and Patient *[[Maya Hansen]] † {{Col-2}} ===Enemies=== *[[Ten Rings (Organization)|Ten Rings]] - Killers **[[Raza Hamidmi al-Wazar]] **[[Abu Bakaar]] **[[Omar (Ten Rings)|Omar]] † **[[Ahmed]] † **[[Khalid]] {{Col-end}} ==Appearances== In chronological order: *{{Appearance|IM3}} (flashbacks) *{{Appearance|IM}} **{{Appearance|IMIAIM1}} **{{Appearance|IMM3ST}} (concurrent events) **{{Appearance|TATAA}} **{{Appearance|IMRASaCD}} **{{Appearance|AIWTHJ}} (flashbacks; concurrent events) ==Trivia== *In the comics, Ho Yinsen was a physicist and engineer from [[w:c:marvel:Timbetpal|Timbetpal]], greatly admired by [[Tony Stark]]. ==References== {{Gallery|Ho Yinsen/Gallery}} {{Wq}} {{Reflist}} ==External Links== *{{Marvel}} *{{WP}} [[de:Ho Yinsen]] [[es:Ho Yinsen]] [[fr:Ho Yinsen]] [[pl:Ho Yinsen]] [[ru:Хо Инсен]] [[uk:Хо Їнсен]] [[zh:伊森]] [[Category:Characters]] [[Category:Iron Man (film) Characters]] [[Category:Iron Man 3 Characters]] [[Category:Comic Characters]] [[Category:Video Game Characters]] [[Category:Book Characters]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Brown Hair]] [[Category:White Hair]] [[Category:Bald Hair]] [[Category:Brown Eyes]] [[Category:Scientists]] [[Category:Physicians]] [[Category:University of Cambridge Students]] [[Category:Multilingual Characters]] [[Category:Deceased Characters]]
